feed.title: 4chan
feed.subtitle: Newest threads from selected worksafe 4chan boards (news, g, o, tv, v, mu, vip) via the read-only JSON API, plus the official 4chan blog. Board posts are user-generated and unmoderated; each title is prefixed with its board.

entry.title: /tv/ TV & Film: so apparently the bluray and streaming versions of the shield are censored, and …
entry.content: so apparently the bluray and streaming versions of the shield are censored, and the definitive version is the old dvd releases. is this true? >The Blu-Ray and Streaming Mistake (Season 3, Episode 5 - "Mum"): When Sony created the high-definition remaster for the Blu-ray release and streaming platforms (like Hulu), they accidentally used a censored international/European master for this specific episode. Because of this, a controversial scene involving David Aceveda is slightly trimmed (cutting a

entry.title: /g/ Technology: why dont they teach computer architecture before data structures
entry.content: i just learned what memory is yesterday explaining an array or linked list is retarded without understanding what memory actually is there's no tangible thing to hold those concepts onto. an array is constant time look up because it just is okay and a linked list you have to start from the head because you just need to okay i feel duped my university doesn't even teach computer architecture its just DSA. all systems classes are optional too. what is the point. i live in america
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /trash/ Off-Topic: /slop/ - AI Slop and Image Generation Thread #3035
entry.content: >Shopping Edition Fapping-focused AIslop thread for those who enjoy plapping to AI thread perfection. A thread for prompters, editors, requesters, gooners, AI pornmommies, PLAPpers, Stable Diffusion directors, NovelAI artists—anything and everything AIslop. No limits past site rules; use Catbox galleries and uploads if needed. Keep dumps to a minimum and don't be a derailing cunt, thanks. Previous Thread: https://desuarchive.org/trash/threa d/84195478/ >Scripts to check image metadata: rentry.or
entry.dc:creator: /trash/ Off-Topic

entry.title: /news/ News: UK Has New Prime Minister: Andy Burnham
entry.content: Burnham vows to ‘bring back hope’ as Britain’s new PM https://www.politico.eu/article/and y-burnham-to-become-uk-prime-minist er/ LONDON — Britain got (another) new prime minister Monday: Andy Burnham. The former mayor of Greater Manchester — who succeeded Keir Starmer as leader of the U.K.’s governing Labour Party last week — will shortly start naming his top team after being asked to form a government by King Charles III. Burnham is Britain’s sixth prime minister in the past decade alone, and
entry.dc:creator: /news/ News

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Level 0 - Use if you need the fastest export and don't care about file size. Lev…
entry.content: Level 0 - Use if you need the fastest export and don't care about file size. Level 6 - Good balance. Commonly the default in many programs. Level 9 - Best if you want the smallest file and don't mind a slower export. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/PNG
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: China’s Moonshot threatens West with ‘full AI communism’
entry.content: >Dean Ball, a senior executive at ChatGPT-developer OpenAI, wrote on X that China’s strategy of giving away its AI code would probably lead to “full AI communism, which is precisely what China proposes”. >Mr Ball suggested that the Trump administration would look to limit the use of Chinese AI models as a result. Alibaba has been blacklisted by the White House because of national security concerns. >A recent report by US company Harmonic Security estimated that one in 12 workers in the US and UK
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /bant/ International/Random: Hello. I post here sometimes about me being a 165cm manlet. It's just that thoug…
entry.content: Hello. I post here sometimes about me being a 165cm manlet. It's just that thoughts about it suddenly fill my head, and I can't get rid of them for days, so I'm trying to talk about it here to hear from anons who have similar problem or just to vent. I try to cope with vidya but even that doesnt help sometimes. That's hard to be so short...
entry.dc:creator: /bant/ International/Random

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Over the years whenever someone expressed an interest in giving Linux a shot, I'…
entry.content: Over the years whenever someone expressed an interest in giving Linux a shot, I'd say >Fuck it, just install Ubuntu But about a year ago I gave Arch and Fedora a shot (on my home PC and laptop respectively) and I was shocked by how everything just works. As it turns out, all the problems I dealt with aren't some kind of "Linux tax" I have to deal with, it's just Ubuntu being ass. But in that case, what do I recommend newbies who want to dip their toes into Linux? Arch has been smooth sailing for
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: Here's my desktop browser tier list.
entry.content: Not included: Ungoogled in S tier, Sliepnir in A tier. Ungoogled Chromium is one of the few browsers that are actually private but doesn't cripple your life in the process. Sliepnir is beautiful and simple. Unfortunately it doesn't work on Linux. Falkon could take it's place if KDE devs actually gave a shit. Thoughts; Pale Moon and Basilisk are awesome because they're both using the independent UXP engine. Might aswell be the same browser, it just depends on which era of Firefox you like more. F
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /g/ Technology: If 1m context length is supposed to be 3000 pages of text literally more than tw…
entry.content: If 1m context length is supposed to be 3000 pages of text literally more than twice the bible then wtf is going on behind the scenes with Anthropic's models for them to constantly have to compact sessions as the 1m context length is reached? I asked Fable 1 thing (in chat mode, where you're able to see all the thinking between the lines) and after 30 minutes or so it had to compact the conversation, I gave it 1 prompt, and I'm pretty sure it did not type several fucking novels
entry.dc:creator: /g/ Technology

entry.title: /int/ International: Pelé played Plumberball "That last brazilian goal in 1970 "wouldn't have happene…
entry.content: Pelé played Plumberball "That last brazilian goal in 1970 "wouldn't have happened today," explains Professor Orlando Laitano of the University of Florida in the United States. He is one of the leading experts in exercise physiology. If that Brazilian team could travel through time, their play would probably be intercepted by modern opponents. And "the biggest imbalance wouldn't be talent, but physiology," according to Laitano."
entry.dc:creator: /int/ International
